# data-model.md

## Naming Conventions

* Field names use **lowerCamelCase**
* All required fields must be present and non-empty
* **`schemaVersion`** is required on every document

---

## Database

* Database name: `goodreadsBooks`

---

## Identifiers

* Each book is uniquely identified by its **Goodreads URL** (`goodreadsUrl`)
* MongoDB `_id` uses the default **ObjectId** generated by MongoDB on insert
* `goodreadsUrl` is enforced as **unique** via a unique index (this is the application-level key — do not use `_id` to look up books)

---

## books Collection

### Document Shape

```json
{
  "_id": "<ObjectId>",
  "goodreadsUrl": "https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/11084145-steve-jobs",
  "title": "Steve Jobs (Hardcover)",
  "author": "Walter Isaacson",
  "avgRating": 4.15,
  "numRatings": 1283171,
  "genres": [
    "biography",
    "business",
    "non-fiction"
  ],
  "genresAutocomplete": [
    "biography",
    "business",
    "non-fiction"
  ],
  "firstSeenGenre": "biography",
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "createdAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

### Field Rules

* `_id` (ObjectId) — MongoDB-generated on insert; not used as application key
* `goodreadsUrl` (string) — required, non-empty, unique
* `title` (string) — required, non-empty; may include edition in parentheses e.g. `"1984 (Paperback)"`
* `author` (string) — required, non-empty
* `avgRating` (number) — range 0.0–5.0, stored as a double
* `numRatings` (int/long) — stored as integer, >= 0
* `genres` (array of strings) — required, non-empty; exact shelf slug strings; no duplicates; no empty strings
* `genresAutocomplete` (array of strings) — mirrors `genres`; used only for prefix-only autocomplete via Atlas Search
* `firstSeenGenre` (string) — required; the genre shelf page that first discovered this book; never updated after insert
* `schemaVersion` (int) — required, for future migrations
* `createdAt`, `updatedAt` (date) — required timestamps

### Insertion / Update Rules

* **On first insert** (new book URL discovered on a genre shelf):
  * `genres` = `[currentGenre]`
  * `genresAutocomplete` = `[currentGenre]`
  * `firstSeenGenre` = `currentGenre`
* **When an existing book URL is found on a new genre shelf**:
  * `$addToSet: { genres: currentGenre, genresAutocomplete: currentGenre }`
  * `$set: { updatedAt: <now> }`
  * All other fields are left unchanged

---

## genres Collection

Canonical set of all known genre slugs. Populated from scrape jobs and the initial migration.

```json
{
  "_id": "non-fiction",
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "createdAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

* `_id` is the genre slug (string) — this is the canonical identifier
* A genre is added here whenever a new scrape job for that genre completes its first page successfully
* Genres are never deleted

---

## genreAliases Collection

Explicit merge mappings: source slug → canonical slug. Managed from the admin panel's **Genre Merges** section (merge/split) and applied at **query/display time only** — never during ingestion.

```json
{
  "_id": "science-fiction",
  "canonical": "sci-fi",
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "createdAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

* `_id` is the **source alias slug** (string) — the raw tag being merged away
* `canonical` is the display/filter target; it does **not** need to exist in the `genres` collection (a brand-new target becomes a merged genre by being pointed at)
* Mappings are flat — an alias can never itself be a merge target (the API rejects chain-creating merges with 409)
* Only explicit admin-defined merges exist here — no automatic normalization
* Raw `genres` arrays on books and the `genres` collection are **never rewritten**; splitting (deleting the alias docs for a canonical) restores the original tags exactly
* Resolution happens in `src/lib/aliases.ts`: `/api/books` expands filters to raw sources and maps facet/result genres to canonicals (deduped per book via `$setUnion`), `/api/genres` lists canonical names

---

## scrapeJobs Collection

Tracks the admin scraping queue and per-job progress.

```json
{
  "_id": "<ObjectId>",
  "genre": "science-fiction",
  "requestedGenre": "science fiction",
  "startPage": 1,
  "currentPage": 7,
  "maxPage": 100,
  "pagesScraped": 6,
  "status": "running",
  "override": false,
  "lastRequestAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z",
  "error": null,
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "createdAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

### Field Notes

* `genre` — the Goodreads shelf slug used in the URL, exactly as typed by the admin (genre merges do not apply to scraping — raw tags are always stored)
* `requestedGenre` — mirrors the admin's input; optional
* `startPage` — the page number the job began from (usually 1, but can differ on manual resume)
* `currentPage` — the next page to be fetched
* `maxPage` — hard cap (100); job stops here even if more pages exist
* `pagesScraped` — count of pages successfully processed
* `lastRequestAt` — timestamp of the last HTTP request made; used to enforce rate limiting across invocations
* `error` — null when healthy; set to an error string or object on failure
* `override` — if true, this job was allowed to run even if the genre had been scraped before

### Status Values

* `queued` — waiting to run
* `running` — actively being processed
* `failed` — stopped due to an error; requires manual retry
* `done` — completed successfully (hit a stop condition or maxPage)

---

## appConfig Collection

Stores global admin-managed runtime configuration. Single document.

```json
{
  "_id": "global",
  "goodreadsCookie": "<raw cookie string>",
  "rateLimitMs": 3000,
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "updatedAt": "2025-12-22T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

* `goodreadsCookie` — the session cookie string used for all scraping requests; set and refreshed via admin UI
* `rateLimitMs` — minimum milliseconds between Goodreads requests; the app falls back to 10,000 when unset; not an environment variable

---

## Notes

* `/api/books` is two-path: **Atlas Search only when a text query is present**; genre/rating-only filtering uses a plain aggregation. Genre facet counts always come from a regular aggregation, never Atlas Search
* Genre merges (`genreAliases`) are resolved at query/display time; stored data is always raw
* Target dataset size: ~100MB, up to ~300k books
* `genresAutocomplete` always mirrors `genres` exactly — they are kept in sync on every write
